summary: The current app uses the long-standing CTF language-bar user preference path and writes ShowStatus = 3 to hide the language bar. A Microsoft Q&A thread now documents ShowStatus = 3 for the hidden state, so the record can be validated.

setting.name: Language Bar Visibility
setting.casual_explanation: The language bar shows keyboard or input-language controls. This tweak hides that UI for the current user.
setting.why_people_change_it: People hide it when they use a single keyboard layout or want a cleaner taskbar and desktop.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Hiding it reduces UI clutter, but it also removes a visible way to switch or inspect input-language settings from that bar.
setting.professional_notes: The current app writes HKCU\Software\Microsoft\CTF\LangBar\ShowStatus = 3. Repo visibility notes map ShowStatus values as 0 = Floating on desktop, 3 = Hidden, and 4 = Docked in the taskbar. A Microsoft Q&A thread also shows ShowStatus = 3 as the hidden-state value.

allowed_values.label: Floating on desktop
allowed_values.meaning: Repo notes say the language bar is shown as a floating bar on the desktop.

allowed_values.label: Hidden
allowed_values.meaning: Repo notes say the language bar is hidden.

allowed_values.label: Docked in taskbar
allowed_values.meaning: Repo notes say the language bar is docked in the taskbar.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app hides the language bar by writing ShowStatus = 3 for the current user.

windows_defaults.label: User preference baseline unknown
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows users with the language bar available

states.rationale: This review did not establish one official Microsoft-published default for the ShowStatus registry value.

windows_defaults.notes: The feature is real, but the exact registry default remains unsourced from a primary Microsoft registry publication in this review.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows current UI
intended_for: People who may switch input languages often
avoid_for: Users who strongly want the language bar hidden

states.rationale: Do not publish a validated default until the registry baseline is sourced more cleanly.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to avoid forcing an unsourced registry baseline.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You explicitly want the language bar hidden today.
tradeoffs: The record stays honest about the missing primary-source default.
The current app behavior remains documented but not validated.

recommended_profiles.label: Hide language bar
intended_for: People who want a cleaner UI
avoid_for: Users who rely on the language bar to switch layouts

states.rationale: Use the repo-documented hidden value that the current app writes.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You intentionally want the language bar hidden.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need an officially documented registry mapping before applying changes.
tradeoffs: The language bar is hidden for the current user.
The mapping is repo-backed in this review, not primary-source-backed.

evidence.title: Microsoft Q&A: language bar ShowStatus hidden-state example
evidence.summary: A Microsoft Q&A thread shows ShowStatus = 3 for the CTF language bar hidden state.

evidence.title: Repo visibility notes for language bar
evidence.summary: The repo documents ShowStatus values for HKCU\Software\Microsoft\CTF\LangBar as 0 = Floating on desktop, 3 = Hidden, and 4 = Docked in the taskbar.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes ShowStatus = 3 under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\CTF\LangBar for the current tweak.

decision.why: The feature mapping is corroborated by the repo notes and a Microsoft Q&A thread that shows ShowStatus = 3 as the hidden-state value.

validation_proof.notes: Validated against a Microsoft Q&A thread that explicitly shows ShowStatus = 3 for the hidden language-bar state.
